highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.193 Released
highlights.summary: This release adds the `autoMode.classifyAllShell` setting, which routes all Bash and PowerShell commands through the auto-mode classifier for more consistent permission handling. It also improves transparency by adding denial reasons to the transcript, toast notifications, and the `/permissions` menu so you always know why a command was blocked.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.191 Released
highlights.summary: Version 2.1.191 introduces `/rewind`, a handy command that lets you resume a conversation from before you ran `/clear`, saving you from losing your session context. It also fixes two quality-of-life bugs: scroll position no longer jumps to the bottom while reading earlier output, and stopped background agents no longer unexpectedly restart.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.186 Released
highlights.summary: This release adds `claude mcp login <name>` and `claude mcp logout <name>` CLI commands so you can authenticate MCP servers directly from the terminal without opening the interactive menu — including over SSH with `--no-browser`. It also adds status filtering to the `/workflows` agent view, making it easier to manage multiple running agents.

highlights.title: The text in Claude Code's "Extended Thinking" output is not authentic
highlights.summary: This article argues that the visible "thinking" text shown during Claude Code's Extended Thinking mode is not a genuine trace of the model's internal reasoning process, raising questions about transparency and trust. Developers relying on this output to understand or audit Claude's decision-making should be aware it may be more of a narrative summary than a literal thought log.

highlights.title: Show HN: Recall – Local project memory for Claude Code
highlights.summary: Recall is an open-source tool that gives Claude Code persistent, local memory about your project, so it remembers context across sessions without you having to re-explain things each time. This is especially useful for long-running projects where re-establishing context wastes time and tokens.

highlights.title: From Zero to Claude Code in 19 Minutes (no code)
highlights.summary: This beginner-friendly YouTube tutorial walks you through getting started with Claude Code in under 20 minutes, requiring no prior coding experience. It's a great starting point if you want a fast, practical introduction before diving into more advanced workflows.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 minutes
highlights.summary: This YouTube video offers a concise but comprehensive overview of Claude Code, targeting developers who already have the basics down and want to level up their workflow quickly. In 30 minutes it covers key features and patterns to help you work more efficiently.

highlights.title: Ask HN: Anthropic banned me from using Claude Code and I don't know what to do
highlights.summary: A developer shares their experience of being banned from Claude Code twice — first while using a VPN, and again when re-signing up with the same credit card — highlighting how automated fraud detection can catch legitimate users. If you're using Claude Code on a personal account, it's worth knowing that VPNs and reused payment details may trigger account restrictions.

tips_of_the_day: If you accidentally run `/clear` and lose your session context, use the new `/rewind` command to jump back to before the clear and pick up right where you left off.
Avoid using a VPN when signing up for or using Claude Code on a personal account — automated fraud detection can flag VPN traffic and lead to unexpected account restrictions.

Enable `autoMode.classifyAllShell` in your Claude Code settings to get consistent auto-mode permission handling for all shell commands, not just those that match arbitrary-code-execution patterns — this reduces surprising permission prompts mid-workflow.
